response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role demands hardware-centric expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. His low-latency DLang work and C/C++ experience at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but there is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization or silicon-level performance architecture, making the fit weak for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, millions of requests/sec)
Broad technical range across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: Strong general systems and low-latency experience
